L1.area: Deep link handling and event dispatch logic in the mobile platform plugins

L2.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data from a specially constructed deep link URL is concatenated directly into a JavaScript string that is then evaluated in the application's web view context, without any sanitization or validation. This allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript code within any application that processes deep links through the plugin.

L3.area: Android platform plugin deep link intake and JavaScript bridge event dispatch
L3.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data from a specially constructed deep link URL is concatenated directly into a JavaScript string that is then evaluated in the application's web view context, without any sanitization or validation. On the Android platform, when the plugin receives an intent with ACTION_VIEW, the URI data is embedded unsanitized into a JavaScript call dispatched to the Cordova web view, allowing a remote attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript code.

ground_truth.reason: Deep link URL data is concatenated directly into a JavaScript string and evaluated in the Cordova web view without sanitization, enabling arbitrary script injection via a crafted deep link.
